State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission

P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement • • Statements of Economic Interest
(916) 322-5662        322-5660        322-5901        322-6441        322-6444

November 22, 1983


Sharon J. Brown
De Anza Real Estate
4300 Appian Way
El Sobrante, CA  94803

                                        Re:  Your Request for Advice, Our
                                             Advice No. A-83-241

Dear Ms. Brown:

     Thank you for your request for advice on the conflict of
interest provisions of the Political Reform Act.  The facts, as
stated in your letter and during our telehone conversation, are
that you are considering running for a San Pablo City Council
seat.  You are a real estate agent who has an independent
contractor relationship with De Anza Real Estate in El Sobrante,
California.1/  The majority of your business is accomplished
outside of the City of San Pablo and is primarily focused on
single family units.  You have asked for an explanation of how
the conflict of interest provisions will apply if you are
successful in your council bid.

     Government Code Section 87100 2/ provides that no local
public official shall make, participate in the making, or in any
way attempt to use her position to influence,3/ a governmental
decision in which she knows or has reason to know she has a
financial interest.  An official has a "financial interest" in a
decision within the meaning of Section 87100 if it is reasonably

___________________
     1/  Your position is known as a "Realtor Associate."

     2/  Hereinafter all statutory references are to the
Government Code unless otherwise indicated.

     3/  For the definition of "make, participate in the
making, or in any way attempting to use his position to
influence," see the enclosed copy of 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section
13700.

Sharon J. Brown
November 22, 1983
Page 2


foreseeable that the decision will have a material financial
effect,1/ distinguishable from the effect on the public
generally,3/ on:

* * *

(c) Any source of income . . . aggregating $250
or more in value provided to, received by or promised
to the public official within 12 months prior to the
time when the decision was made....

Section 87103(c).

Assuming you are elected, the real estate agency with whom
you have an independent contractor relationship will be a source
of income to you. In addition, the buyer, seller, or both buyer
and seller of property will be a source of income to you,
depending upon whom you represented in a particular transaction,
if you received or became entitled to $250 or more in fees
within the prior 12 months.

As a city councilmember, you will be required to disqualify
yourself from making, participating in the making, or in any way
attempting to use your position to influence a decision5/
which will foreseeably have a material financial effect on the
agency or any buyer or seller who is a source of income to you.
The fact that the majority of your business is accomplished
outside, rather than inside, the City of San Pablo may limit, to
some extent, the number of decisions that will require your
disqualification because it makes it less likely that your
sources of income will be materially affected by decisions
before the San Pablo City Council.


4/ For an explanation of the phrase "material financial
effect," see the enclosed copy of 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section
18702.

5/ For an explanation of the "public generally"
exception, see the enclosed copy of 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section
18703.

6/ I have included copies of the Harron and Thacher
advice letters which discuss the phrase "using his position to
influence."

Sharon J. Brown
November 22, 1983
Page 3


     You will determine whether disqualification is required on a
decision-by-decision basis. If, on a particular decision, you
are unable to determine whether disqualification is required,
you can contact our office for advice at 916/322-5901.

                                        Very truly yours,

                                        [Signature]
                                        Janis Shank McLean
                                        Counsel, Legal Division

JSM:km

DE ANZA REAL ESTATE
4300 APPIAN WAY                    223-7171                EL SOBRANTE, CALIFORNIA 94803
                OCT 24 1983         BROKER: Bernelle Johnson


                                                           October 18, 1983




Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, California


Dear Sir:

     I am concerned about the ramifications with regard to the attached
article that appeared in the Oakland Tribune. I am a Realtor Associate
with De Anza Real Estate in El Sobrante, California and am considering
running for the San Pablo City Council. The majority of my business is
accomplished outside the city of San Pablo and deals primarily with single
family units.

     As I do not deal with "developers" or represent clients of that mag-
nitude, I am hopeful there will be no "Conflict of Interest" but I will
need some assurance of that fact.

     I am sure this subject will surface during the campaign and would
therefore appreciate any comments the Commission might be able to make
with regard to the subject.

     I hope to hear from you in the near future and appreciate your
assistance.

                                                           Sincerely,

                                                           Sharon J Brown
                                                           Sharon J Brown

Encl.

Section B

The Tr

ollegiate

INSIDE
Brenda Payton . . . . . . . . . B-2
Editorials . . . . . . . . . . . . . . B-6
Commentary . . . . . . . . . . B-7

Dublin councilman
Burton resigns

Business interests as a real estate
agent conflicted with official duties

By John Miller
The Tribune

DUBLIN — Dublin City Councilman David Burton resigned
Tuesday because he said he can't
function as a city official while
working as a real estate agent,
and criticized state laws which
he said make it increasingly difficult for many people like him
to hold public office.

"I couldn't even ask a simple
question at City Hall," Burton
said after announcing his resignation. "According to the Fair
Political Practices Commission,
I can't even talk to any member
of the city staff or other city
agencies and represent my client too."

Burton, a retired corporation
executive and part-time college
instructor, represents two
clients who have development
projects before the city. He has
abstained from voting on these
projects, and on a master plan
for development of the area
west of San Ramon Road, because his clients own land in the
area.

Burton's inability to vote on
these matters has resulted in
some 2-2 votes among remaining council members, creating a
deadlock. Burton said if he remained on the council there
could be similar deadlocks in the
future, "and that would be unfair."

He also said his inability to
zealously pursue his clients' interests could cost them thousands of dollars.

Burton, an early and ardent
supporter of Dublin cityhood and
[illegible] by [illegible] biggest boost

David Burton
Criticized state laws

members Oct. 3 of his intention
to resign. He had not planned to
run for re-election next April in
any case.

His decision to step down
came after he learned of a state
Fair Political Practices Commission ruling in a Chula Vista
case in which the city was notified that a councilman, a planning commissioner and a prospective official, who were either
attorneys or real estate agents
representing clients doing business with the city, should not be
permitted even to talk to the
city staff or the public about
their clients' projects, much less
vote on them.

See RESIGN, Page B-4

/

State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement • • Statements of Economic Interest
(916) 322-5662 322-5660 322-5901 322-4441 322-6444

October 25, 1983


Sharon J. Brown
De Anza Real Estate
4300 Appian Way
El Sobrante, CA 94803
Re: A-83-241

Dear Ms. Brown:

Your letter requesting advice under the Political
Reform Act has been referred to Janis Shank McLean, an
attorney in the Legal Division of the Fair Political
Practices Commission. If you have any questions about your
advice request, you may contact this attorney directly at
(916) 322-5901.

We try to answer all advice requests promptly.
Therefore, unless your request poses particularly complex
legal questions, or unless more information is needed to
answer your request, you should expect a response within 21
working days.

Very truly yours,

[illegible]
Barbara A. Wilman
Chief, Legal Division

sections.conclusion: As a city councilmember, you will be required to disqualify yourself from making, participating in the making, or in any way attempting to use your position to influence a decision which will foreseeably have a material financial effect on the agency or any buyer or seller who is a source of income to you. The fact that the majority of your business is accomplished outside, rather than inside, the City of San Pablo may limit, to some extent, the number of decisions that will require your disqualification because it makes it less likely that your sources of income will be materially affected by decisions before the San Pablo City Council. You will determine whether disqualification is required on a decision-by-decision basis.
sections.question_synthetic: A prospective San Pablo city council candidate who works as a real estate agent seeks guidance on how California's conflict of interest laws will restrict her official duties if elected, particularly regarding decisions affecting her real estate business and clients.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The applicant must disqualify herself from decisions that foreseeably have a material financial effect on her real estate business or clients if they are sources of income aggregating $250+ in the prior 12 months. Her focus on business outside San Pablo will likely limit disqualifications. Determinations will be made on a case-by-case basis with FPPC available for consultation.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: ocr; LLM: Document contains both the initial incoming request (October 18, 1983 letter from Brown) and the FPPC response (November 22, 1983 advice letter from Janis Shank McLean, plus an October 25 acknowledgment letter). Extracted information from the main advice letter response. OCR quality is good with minor formatting irregularities. Included newspaper article about Dublin councilman Burton's resignation over similar conflict of interest concerns provides context but is not part of FPPC's formal response.

CONCLUSION: As a city councilmember, you will be required to disqualify yourself from making, participating in the making, or in any way attempting to use your position to influence a decision which will foreseeably have a material financial effect on the agency or any buyer or seller who is a source of income to you. The fact that the majority of your business is accomplished outside, rather than inside, the City of San Pablo may limit, to some extent, the number of decisions that will require your disqualification because it makes it less likely that your sources of income will be materially affected by decisions before the San Pablo City Council. You will determine whether disqualification is required on a decision-by-decision basis.

embedding.summary: FPPC provides advice to a prospective San Pablo city council candidate regarding conflict of interest restrictions under Government Code Section 87100-87103, explaining that she must disqualify herself from decisions affecting her real estate business and clients, though her focus outside San Pablo may limit the scope of required disqualifications.
